source.catalog_ref: HV23 (user-assigned; catalog HV22 Global Hyperloop Competition 2.0 IIT Madras - flagged: user's label noted 'combat robots' but catalog entry is hyperloop-class pod demo)

full_prompt: Design a monolithic 7020-T6 aluminum primary chassis frame for a Global Hyperloop Competition 2.0 (collegiate-class IIT Madras) 2026 student pod, running on the IIT Madras 422 m hyperloop test track under partial-vacuum (target tube pressure ~100 mbar = 10 kPa absolute) with a linear-induction-motor propulsion system. The chassis frame is the primary structural spine carrying the pod's battery pack (80 kg), LIM reaction plate (25 kg), magnetic-levitation permanent-magnet arrays (2 x 15 kg), vacuum-rated shell (60 kg), and a crew-simulation ballast (0 kg, pod is unmanned for collegiate-class 2.0 per competition rules). The frame is a single billet-machined + FSW-welded (treated as monolithic for this prompt; the two FSW seams modeled as continuous base-metal equivalent) 7020-T6 part. Geometry: ladder-style frame 2200 mm long x 600 mm wide x 120 mm tall; two integral 80 mm x 40 mm x 4 mm wall rectangular-section longerons along the 2200 mm length, spaced 500 mm apart (centerline); five integral 60 mm x 40 mm x 3 mm wall rectangular cross-members at 0 / 550 / 1100 / 1650 / 2200 mm stations. Four integral magnet-array mounting pads on the underside of the two outer longerons at 400 / 800 / 1400 / 1800 mm stations: each pad 100 mm x 80 mm x 8 mm with 4 M10 (11 mm clearance) through-holes on a 70 x 50 mm pattern. Two integral battery-tray mounting flanges on the upper longerons between stations 550-1100 and 1100-1650: each flange 500 mm long x 40 mm wide x 6 mm thick with 6 M8 (9 mm clearance) holes on a 250 mm pitch x 2 rows. One integral LIM reaction-plate mounting pad centered at 1100 mm station: 400 x 200 x 6 mm with 8 M10 through-holes. Overall bounding envelope 2200 x 600 x 120 mm. Material 7020-T6 (E 71 GPa, nu 0.33, density 2780 kg/m^3, yield 290 MPa, UTS 350 MPa, endurance 90 MPa at 5e8 cycles, weldable). Three load cases per hyperloop-class pod collegiate-class 2.0 design practice. Load Case 1 (full propulsion at 0.5g longitudinal accel): pod total accelerated mass ~200 kg x 0.5 x 9.81 = 980 N longitudinal thrust applied at the LIM reaction pad, battery 80 kg + magnets 2 x 15 kg + shell 60 kg distributed as distributed weight loads, 4 magnet-pads constrained as levitation-reaction bearings (vertical + lateral constraint). Load Case 2 (emergency braking at 2g): 3920 N decelerating longitudinal load at the LIM pad + brake skid pads, all masses inertially loaded at 2g forward (brake-direction); same magnet-pad constraints. Load Case 3 (vacuum-pull-down differential + thermal): 10 kPa differential pressure acting on the 1 m^2 bottom-projected area of the pod shell transmitted through the frame as distributed 10 kN upward body load; plus thermal expansion differential 25 C (tube wall 20 C, chassis 45 C after 2 km run) causing 0.3 mm axial growth that is restrained by the LIM-pad bolt pattern. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 propulsion accel, peak von Mises must not exceed 145 MPa (yield/2.0); (R2) under LC2 emergency 2g braking, peak stress must not exceed 193 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) under LC3 vacuum differential + thermal, peak stress must not exceed 193 MPa; (R4) first six natural frequencies (frame + 80 kg battery + 30 kg magnets + 25 kg LIM lumped at their pads) must exceed 25 Hz to decouple from the 5-15 Hz LIM inverter switching ripple and the 3-8 Hz track-joint excitation at up to 100 m/s pod speed on 5 m track-panel spacing; (R5) frame mass must not exceed 35 kg (collegiate-class 2.0 competitive pods target chassis <= 20% of 180 kg total pod mass); (R6) a linear buckling analysis of the longerons under LC2 braking compressive flow (pod braking reacts into longeron-front through inertia load path) must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 3.0; (R7) maximum deflection of the battery-tray mounting flange under LC2 must not exceed 2 mm (preserves battery cell pack alignment to prevent internal busbar stress). Thermal analysis reduced to equivalent thermal growth in LC3; fluid and radiation not required (tube convection negligible at 100 mbar). Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 7020-T6 aluminum primary chassis frame for a collegiate Global Hyperloop Competition 2.0 student pod (IIT Madras 422 m test track at ~10 kPa absolute, LIM propulsion, unmanned). The frame is a single billet-machined part with two FSW seams treated as base-metal equivalent, laid out as a ladder with two 80 x 40 x 4 mm RHS longerons and five 60 x 40 x 3 mm cross-members, four magnet-array mounting pads under the outer longerons, two battery-tray flanges on the upper longerons, and a central LIM reaction-plate pad. Apply yield/2.0 continuous and yield/1.5 transient safety factors.

Envelope: 2200 x 600 x 120 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 full propulsion at 0.5 g longitudinal: ~200 kg total accelerated mass = 980 N thrust at the LIM pad, payloads distributed (battery 80 kg, magnets 2 x 15 kg, shell 60 kg), magnet pads constrained as levitation bearings.
- LC2 emergency 2 g braking: 3920 N decel load at LIM + skid pads with all masses inertially loaded at 2 g forward.
- LC3 vacuum pull-down + thermal: 10 kPa differential on the 1 m^2 bottom projected area (10 kN upward) plus a 25 C thermal gradient restrained at the LIM-pad bolt pattern.
Also check first six modes >25 Hz (clear 5-15 Hz inverter ripple and 3-8 Hz track-joint), a linear-buckling factor >=3 under LC2 longeron compression, and battery-tray flange deflection under LC2 below 2 mm.

prompt.title: collegiate-class IIT Madras hyperloop-class pod primary chassis frame
prompt.brief: 7020-T6 ladder frame 2200x600x120 with 2 longerons + 5 cross-members + magnet/battery/LIM mounting pads.

exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Not dominant for short run times.
exclusions_explained.interpretation_flag: User's HV23 label said 'combat robots (collegiate-class IIT Madras)' but catalog entry is 'Global Hyperloop Competition 2.0'; followed catalog interpretation.

eval_coverage: collegiate-class 2.0 propulsion + braking + vacuum triple LC
